behavior_in_task_description.reason: All test assertions check for patterns explicitly described in instruction.md: the vector<pair<String,int32_t>> type, emplace_back with two args, tryRemove with version, ZBADVERSION handling alongside ZNONODE, removal counting with 'Removed {}/{} stale processing nodes', LOG_TRACE messages for removal and skip, the function name cleanupPersistentProcessingNodes, and the file path. Every asserted string/pattern is discoverable from the instruction.

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly tells the agent exactly what to change: replace 'Strings nodes_to_remove' with 'std::vector<std::pair<String, int32_t>>', pass version to tryRemove, handle ZBADVERSION alongside ZNONODE, use specific log format strings like 'Removed {}/{} stale processing nodes' and 'Removing stale processing node: {}'. This is essentially a line-by-line specification of the fix, not just a description of the bug.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on gold-specific implementation details: test_version_in_nodes_to_remove requires exactly std::vector<std::pair<String, int32_t>>, test_node_and_version_extraction requires .first/.second or structured bindings, test_removal_tracking requires the exact log format 'Removed {}/{} stale processing nodes'. An alternative using a struct instead of pair, or a map, or different log formatting would fail despite being correct.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Every test reads the source file as text and uses regex pattern matching (re.search on file content). No test compiles the code, executes it, or checks runtime behavior. E.g., test_tryremove_with_version just does re.findall(tryremove_pattern, func_content). This is purely textual assertion.

test_not_tautological.reason: The original file has 'Strings nodes_to_remove' and 'tryRemove(node)' with one argument. A stub/unchanged file would fail tests like test_version_in_nodes_to_remove (no pair declaration), test_tryremove_with_version (tryRemove has one arg), test_zbadversion_handling (no ZBADVERSION), etc. The tests require actual code modifications.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors listed in instruction.md are tested: version storage in pairs (test_version_in_nodes_to_remove), version passed to tryRemove (test_tryremove_with_version), ZBADVERSION handling (test_zbadversion_handling), removal tracking (test_removal_tracking), log before removal (test_log_trace_for_removal), log on skip (test_log_trace_for_skip), and old declaration removal (implicit in requiring the new type).

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named (test_version_in_nodes_to_remove, test_tryremove_with_version, etc.), each has a docstring explaining what behavioral aspect it verifies, and they are logically grouped by the aspect of the fix they validate.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task modifies C++ source code, not structured data output like JSON/CSV/API/DB.

no_typos.reason: File paths, function names, variable names, and error codes are all correctly spelled throughout instruction.md and test files.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: instruction.md names the exact file (src/Storages/ObjectStorageQueue/ObjectStorageQueueMetadata.cpp), the exact function (cleanupPersistentProcessingNodes), the exact variable to replace ('Strings nodes_to_remove'), and the exact replacement type (std::vector<std::pair<String, int32_t>>). This goes well beyond identifying the buggy area.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task, not an agentmd-edits/config task.
